title: Hygiene in outbreaks/displacement (household-level cholera prevention)
context: Hygiene guidance in outbreak and displacement settings (e.g., cholera prevention at the household level) is a HIGH-risk topic: danger-signs for cholera/severe diarrheal disease must be accurate, and any 'when-to-seek-care' instruction can affect survival. This backlog task is gated on a secured expert reviewer (partner-014) and the CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018), and specifically requires the 'informational, not a substitute for professional care' framing and explicit danger-signs content. It is lower priority than the core M2 units but important for emergency-context deployment.
objective: Author, source-cite, expert-sign-off, and independently numeric-verify a WashUnit covering household-level hygiene behaviors during outbreaks and displacement (focusing on cholera prevention: water treatment, handwashing at critical times, food hygiene, safe excreta disposal in emergency settings), with required danger-signs and when-to-seek-care content.
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF/Sphere/IFRC) with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Every numeric value (e.g., any dosing or timing referenced) is independently second-checked and expert-confirmed; expertConfirmed:true on all numericClaims

A recorded expertSignOff from a secured credentialed expert is attached before merge; unit carries explicit danger-signs/when-to-seek-care content and the required 'informational, not a substitute for professional medical care' disclaimer
Scope is enforced: household preventive behaviors only; no clinical treatment instruction; borderline cases linked to care rather than instructed
CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018) passes: expertSignOff present, all numericClaims verified and expertConfirmed:true, disclaimer and danger-signs fields present
Reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author and the expert; PR-tied review-log entry written
output: A committed, expert-signed-off, independently numeric-verified WashUnit for household-level hygiene in outbreaks/displacement (cholera prevention), with danger-signs, disclaimer, and PR-tied review-log entry.
